calendar.matters: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Order Setting Default Aside (Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declarations Of Geoffrey Murry, Jesus Alejandro Montiel And Thomas E. Smurro In Support Thereof Filed Separately)
Off Calendar May-18-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JESUS MONTIEL, AKA JESUS ALEJANDRO MONTIEL URBINA, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS A 50% MEMBER OF SAN FRANCISCO EAGLE BAR LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S Motion To Reconsider Order Setting Default Aside.

Plaintiff Jesus Montiel's Motion To Reconsider Order Setting Default Aside is DENIED.

Plaintiff moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a). Section 1008(a) permits reconsideration of a prior order when the moving party shows "new or different facts, circumstances, or law" and explains why the new or different facts, circumstances, or law were not presented earlier with reasonable diligence. (New York Times Co. v. Superior Court (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 206, 208.) "The party making the application shall state by affidavit what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." (Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a).)

Plaintiff here has not identified new or different facts, circumstances, or law that could not have been presented earlier with reasonable diligence that justify reconsideration of the court's 3/4/2026 Order. Plaintiff seeks to overcome the court's finding that he failed to demonstrate reasonable diligence before sub-serving Plaintiff. First, Plaintiff appears to argue his motion should have been granted based on the Murry Declaration, Ex. 5, filed on 2/19/2026. This evidence was before the court at the time it decided the underlying default/quash motion; it is in no way new. Plaintiff, in fact, presents a new argument to support a finding that he exercised reasonable diligence before attempting substitute service of the FAC. A new argument is not a basis for reconsideration. Second, Plaintiff claims he is presenting a new proof of service from LASD. He claims the new POS is attached as Exhibit 15 to the 3/16/2026 Murry Declaration. The 3/16/2026 Murry Declaration has no attachments. Plaintiff also refers to Exhibits 16 and 17 of the 3/16/2026 Smurro Declaration. The 3/16/2026 Smurro Declaration has no attachments. In any event, the shortcoming in the original May 2024 LASD POS re reasonable diligence is obvious on the face of the POS; Plaintiff should have exercised due diligence by revieing the May 2024 LASD POS when he received it and pursuing correction long before the 3/4/2026 hearing on Plaintiff's default/quash motion. The court finds none of the evidence submitted on this motion is "new" as that term is used in section 1008(a)

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order that is verbatim to the Court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)

Off Calendar Mar-06-2026 Continued To May-12-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default And Default Judgment And To Dismiss Action For Failure To Bring To Trial Within Five Years, And For Violation Of Statute Of Limitations, And To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Mar-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Prir to the hearing, the notice to contest email by Defense is withdrawn.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Leon's motion to vacate the default entered on 6/25/24 is GRANTED.

Defendant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(d). Section 473(d) provides: "The court may, upon motion of the injured party, or its own motion, correct clerical mistakes in its judgment or orders as entered, so as to conform to the judgment or order directed, and may, on motion of either party after notice to the other party, set aside any void judgment or order."

Plaintiff fails to show that the first amended complaint was properly substitute served on defendant and the default is void. To effectuate substitute service under Code of Civil Procedure section 415.20(b), plaintiff must demonstrate inter alia reasonable diligence in attempting to personally serve the defendant. Here, the proof of service only shows one attempt to serve the first amended complaint personally before resorting to substitute service. (5/1/24 Proof of Service.) One attempt is insufficient and plaintiff fails to show substantial compliance. (See Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 1387, 1391-1392 [" ' "Ordinarily, ... two or three attempts at personal service at a proper place should fully satisfy the requirement of reasonable diligence and allow substituted service to be made." ' "].)

The proof of service also refers to service attempts from the Sheriff. But that certification indicates that the Sheriff was attempting to serve the original complaint and summons, not the first amended complaint. That certification therefore fails to show reasonable diligence. The court declines Plaintiff's invitation to draw inferences contrary to the plaint text of the Sheriff's proof.

Defendant's merits-based arguments to dismiss this action are premature. A motion to vacate default is not the proper vehicle to raise such arguments.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
